cards.title: Having children makes you smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. The mental and emotional gymnastics of parenting appear to boost cognitive health.

cards.title: Coping with worry on family holidays
cards.body: Anxiety about worst-case scenarios can overshadow family time, but real emergencies reveal our capacity to cope. Learning to manage worry helps parents and children relax and enjoy time together.

cards.title: What is mindful parenting?
cards.body: Mindful parenting helps when emotions override your calm and you react to children in ways you regret. Three simple steps can help you maintain equilibrium during challenging moments.

cards.title: Help children through divorce
cards.body: How parents handle divorce significantly shapes their child's future resilience and adjustment. Practical, age-specific strategies enable children to navigate family change with confidence.

cards.title: Growing children by stepping back
cards.body: Free play remains one of the most important foundations of healthy child development. Stepping back allows children to develop independence and creativity naturally.

cards.title: When adult children blame parents
cards.body: Adult children blaming parents for struggles can trap families in guilt and rescuing patterns. Three effective strategies help parents respond constructively and promote independence.

cards.title: Benedict's Law overhauls school allergy training
cards.body: New legislation will improve allergy training in schools following a child's death from accidental allergen exposure. The changes aim to prevent similar tragedies.
